title: genista mugronensis subsp. mugronensis
intro: Genista mugronensis subsp. mugronensis, also known as the Mugron broom, is a subspecies of broom shrub native to western France, characterized by its yellow flowers and small, needle-like leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin issues, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Genista mugronensis subsp. mugronensis include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and act as an expectorant to relieve congestion. Genista mugronensis subsp. mugronensis can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
